pr.title: Finishing labeling of Character Data pointers
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Labeled remaining character data pointers in asm/data.s by replacing raw embedded addresses with symbolic labels and converting hex-encoded string data for Master Hand and Crazy Hand character data into explicit .asciz strings with alignment. The PR also added missing global labels for referenced data blocks so nearby pointer tables could refer to symbols instead of numeric addresses.
what_changed: Replaced raw .4byte address constants such as 0x803D41F8, 0x803D35E8, 0x803D4070, 0x803D407C, 0x803D4818, 0x803D4824, 0x803D34A8, 0x803D40C4, 0x803D4868, 0x803D40B8, and 0x803D485C with symbolic labels.

Added global labels for previously unlabeled data targets, including lbl_803D34A8, lbl_803D4070, lbl_803D407C, lbl_803D4090, lbl_803D409C, lbl_803D40B8, lbl_803D40C4, lbl_803D41F8, lbl_803D4818, lbl_803D4824, lbl_803D4834, lbl_803D4840, lbl_803D485C, and lbl_803D4868.
Decoded Master Hand file and archive strings from raw word values into .asciz directives: "PlMh.dat", "ftDataMasterhand", "PlMhNr.dat", "PlyMasterhand_Share_joint", and "PlMhAJ.dat".
Decoded Crazy Hand file and archive strings from raw word values into .asciz directives: "PlCh.dat", "ftDataCrazyhand", "PlChNr.dat", "PlyCrazyhand_Share_joint", and "PlChAJ.dat".

Converted small pointer-list structures for Master Hand and Crazy Hand costume/resource data from raw address words into label references, e.g. lbl_803D40C4 now points at lbl_803D4090 and lbl_803D409C, and lbl_803D4868 now points at lbl_803D4834 and lbl_803D4840.
Preserved .balign 4 after newly introduced .asciz string literals to maintain the original word-aligned data layout.
smart_moves: Used symbolic label references in pointer tables rather than hard-coded absolute addresses, making the data relocatable and easier to cross-reference during later decompilation.
Decoded obvious ASCII-in-hex data into .asciz strings, improving readability without changing the underlying data intent.
Added .global labels at targets before replacing address literals, ensuring the new symbolic references had concrete named destinations.
Kept explicit .balign 4 after strings, which is important because the original data was word-oriented .4byte content and subsequent fields must remain aligned.
Grouped related labels around the existing Master Hand and Crazy Hand data regions, preserving locality between character file names, data identifiers, and pointer-list structures.
decomp_lessons: When a .4byte value points into the same data section and the target is identifiable, prefer replacing the raw address with a label rather than leaving an absolute constant.
Hex .4byte sequences that decode to readable asset names should be converted to .asciz plus alignment when the bytes represent NUL-terminated strings.
For data matching, converting string words to .asciz requires checking padding and alignment; .balign 4 is needed when later fields are word pointers or integers.

Character data tables often mix pointers to strings, pointers to pointer arrays, NULL terminators, and numeric fields; labeling each referenced region makes the structure clearer before C struct recovery.
Boss fighter data such as Master Hand and Crazy Hand follows the same general asset naming pattern as playable character data: character .dat, ftData name, Nr .dat, shared joint name, and AJ .dat.
naming_conventions: Existing auto labels retain the address-derived form lbl_803Dxxxx for anonymous data symbols.
Decoded asset file names use game-internal capitalization such as "PlMh.dat", "PlMhNr.dat", "PlMhAJ.dat", "PlCh.dat", "PlChNr.dat", and "PlChAJ.dat".
Decoded fighter data identifiers use strings such as "ftDataMasterhand" and "ftDataCrazyhand".
Shared joint resource strings use the pattern "Ply<Name>_Share_joint", shown by "PlyMasterhand_Share_joint" and "PlyCrazyhand_Share_joint".
The PR did not introduce semantic C-style symbol names beyond existing lbl_ address labels; it focused on labeling raw addresses and making strings explicit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace `.4byte 0x803Dxxxx` with `.4byte lbl_803Dxxxx` when the value is a data pointer and the target label exists or can be introduced.
Introduce `.global lbl_803Dxxxx` immediately before data that is externally referenced by pointer tables.
Convert big-endian ASCII word sequences such as `0x506C4D68 0x2E646174 0x00000000` into `.asciz "PlMh.dat"` followed by `.balign 4`.
For pointer subarrays embedded after string data, label the subarray and emit symbolic `.4byte` entries followed by NULL terminators to preserve layout.
Use labels for address constants inside character-data tables to make subsequent struct inference and data migration from asm to C easier.
searchable_terms: Finishing labeling of Character Data pointers
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace lbl_803D4070-style labels with semantic character data struct names.
Search for other hex-encoded asset strings in asm/data.s that can be converted to .asciz with .balign 4.
Inspect character data tables around lbl_803C12E0, lbl_803C1F40, and lbl_803C2360 for struct layout recovery opportunities.
Compare Master Hand and Crazy Hand data blocks with playable fighter character data blocks to infer shared table formats.
